preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: Startups needing an AI-enabled design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Always first. Establishes context for startup audiences exploring an AI-enabled design system, framing what MODE is and who it serves — critical for the Explorer archetype who needs clear grounding before deeper engagement.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Archetype guidance requires Value for Explorers. At the decision funnel stage, surfacing product capabilities and outcome promises gives the Explorer substantive reasons to consider committing, satisfying their discovery-oriented mindset before seeing proof.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Archetype guidance requires Credibility for Explorers. Trust signals and social proof from other startups validate MODE's claims, reducing perceived risk for a decision-stage visitor who is comparing options.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Decision funnel stage requires Evidence before Conversion. Concrete metrics, case study data, and proof points give the Explorer the empirical backing they need to feel confident moving toward a purchase decision.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Decision funnel stage requires the Decision beat before Conversion. Presenting pricing and plan comparisons frames the final choice clearly, respecting the Explorer's need for transparency without applying undue pressure.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Must appear immediately before Recovery. Provides the CTA and commitment prompt. Kept low-pressure per Explorer archetype guidance — inviting action rather than forcing it, aligned with their discovery-first mindset.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always last. Offers secondary paths, footer navigation, and exit handling so Explorers who aren't ready to commit can still find value and return later, preserving the relationship.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Always first. Establishes context for startup audiences exploring an AI-enabled design system, framing what MODE is and who it serves — critical for the Explorer archetype who needs clear grounding before deeper engagement.

sections.name: Features
sections.rationale: Archetype guidance requires Value for Explorers. At the decision funnel stage, surfacing product capabilities and outcome promises gives the Explorer substantive reasons to consider committing, satisfying their discovery-oriented mindset before seeing proof.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision-stage conversion page requires minimal variant to 'strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' — the persistent CTA is 'the most converted element on most SaaS pages.'
Explorer archetype 'prefers editorial or with-social-proof — discovery tone, breathing room, no pressure toward conversion' — social proof aligns with organic context and exploratory intent.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE is the semantic intelligence layer that resolves how every component behaves — by archetype, funnel stage, and intent state — without manual variants or configuration overhead.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. Runs in your codebase.

page.reasoning: Explorer variant 'uses 2-col-heavy with longer copy per feature' — supports discovery-focused narrative for intent-aware exploration.

slots.headline: Everything the resolution layer needs to make decisions
slots.subhead: MODE ships as a complete intelligence kit — not a configuration UI, a working system that lives in your repo from day one.

features.title: Semantic resolution engine
features.description: Components resolve behavior across four dimensions — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode — so the right experience is delivered without a single manual variant. The system makes the judgment call; you ship the output.

features.title: IA planner agent
features.description: Go from a one-line brief to a full page architecture in seconds, powered by Claude Opus. The planner selects sections, sequences zones, and assigns component intent before a single line of UI is written.

features.title: Multi-variant generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. Each variant carries its own copy density, evidence weighting, and CTA register — resolved automatically from the behavioral token layer.

features.title: Palette map
features.description: Visual emphasis resolves per component per funnel stage without any manual assignment. The palette map accumulates semantic weight toward conversion, so your UI pulls in the right direction at every scroll depth.

features.title: Component manifest
features.description: Eleven components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent baked in. Every component knows where it belongs on the page and what job it is doing — no guesswork, no drift from intent.

features.title: Full ownership, no lock-in
features.description: MODE lives in your codebase, not a vendor dashboard. Bring your own Anthropic API key, fork the repo, extend the manifest. There is no runtime dependency, no usage-based billing, and no permission required to make it yours.

page.reasoning: Decision-stage funnel requires 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages' — maximizes last-moment conversion opportunity.
